name: component
description: Generate a component (main file, style, test) for React, Vue, Svelte, or Angular — auto-detected from the project's package.json, matching whatever conventions and style library are already in use.

enumDescriptions: Detect the stack from the nearest package.json (falls back to a manual prompt)
componentGenerator.stack.description: Which stack to generate components for.

enumDescriptions: Detect from the nearest package.json's dependencies
Plain CSS/SCSS file (React) or embedded <style> block (Vue/Svelte)
Tailwind utility classes, no separate stylesheet
componentGenerator.styleLib.description: Which styling approach to generate. Vue, Svelte, and Angular only distinguish plain vs Tailwind; the React-specific libraries fall back to plain CSS for them.

enumDescriptions: React: CSS module file imported as styles. Vue/Svelte: plain <style> block. Angular: plain .css file.
React: SCSS module file imported as styles. Vue/Svelte: <style lang="scss"> block. Angular: plain .scss file.
React: plain .css file, side-effect import. Vue/Svelte: plain <style> block. Angular: plain .css file.
React: plain .scss file, side-effect import. Vue/Svelte: <style lang="scss"> block. Angular: plain .scss file.
componentGenerator.styleFormat.description: Stylesheet format. React generates a separate file; Vue and Svelte always embed styles in the component's <style> block; Angular always generates a separate (non-module) file. This setting mainly picks CSS vs SCSS outside of React.

componentGenerator.generateTest.description: Generate a test file alongside the component.

componentGenerator.testFramework.description: Testing library APIs to import in the generated test file.

componentGenerator.fileNaming.description: Naming convention for the main component file inside the generated folder. Angular ignores this and always uses its kebab-case *.component.ts convention.

componentGenerator.exportStyle.description: React only: whether the generated component uses a named export or a default export. Vue and Svelte components are always the file's default export; Angular components are always a named class export.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
